senses.definition: to crown again; to place a crown on someone or something once more
examples: The festival crowned the new queen during the closing ceremony, and the crowd recrowns her with a ceremonial wreath.
The statue was recrowned after restoration.

senses.definition: to crucify again; to condemn or punish severely a second time
examples: The town argued that the old policy would recrucify the survivors by reopening old wounds.
Fans accused the decision of recrucifying a previously settled dispute.

senses.definition: the quality of being recrudescent; the recurrence or renewed revival of something undesirable
examples: The recrudency of the conflict surprised observers.
Medical researchers warned against recrudency of the infection after initial recovery.

senses.definition: to begin to emerge again after a period of dormancy; to revive
examples: The rumor recrudesces every few years in the community.

senses.definition: past tense of recrudesce; having begun to emerge again after a period of dormancy
examples: The issue recrudesced last year after a quiet period.
Tensions recrudesced quickly following the incident.

senses.definition: the renewed outbreak or revival of something that had declined or paused
